title: Split off evaluable/combinational flags in `celltypes.h`
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.34+23 (git sha1 59fbee400, clang++ 11.1.0 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux, macOS, Windows, BSD

### Reproduction Steps

Look into `kernel/celltypes.h` where formal cell types are registered, with the last argument to `setup_type` being whether the cell is evaluable (`CellTypes::cell_evaluable` then returns true for the cell).

https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/27ac91270991ad7c2304e5f90a5c1001c2f08ec1/kernel/celltypes.h#L90-L99

### Expected Behavior

Formal cells like `$assert` are registered as non-evaluable since the cell can have a side effect. This depends on the interpretation of 'evaluable' of course, but I suggest we adopt the definition of "the cell is purely combinational and its output only depends on current inputs", which I think is the most useful definition for writing optimization passes.

### Actual Behavior

Formal cells are registered evaluable. Even if we go with a definition of 'evaluable' for which this is right, I suggest we switch `$print` to evaluable as well (currently it's non-evaluable), since it's very much like `$assert` so this would be the consistent thing to do. The more consistent `CellTypes::cell_evaluable` is, the more useful it is.
